raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I was coerced into an in-person high-pressure sales environment at a skincare shop operating under the name XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX in XXXX XXXX Colorado. However, they are a branch of the company " XXXX XXXX. '' The staff overwhelmed me with rapid demonstrations and encouraged immediate purchases without clearly disclosing prices or terms. I was never shown a total before the transactions were processed, nor was I given a receipt until after I noticed charges on my credit card totaling {$3800.00} and {$210.00}. 

I returned to the store within XXXX hours to request a refund. The staff refused, stating all sales are final and claiming only the owner could approve refundsbut the owner was out of town indefinitely. They also refused to accept the unopened products when I attempted to return them. 

Following this, the business repeatedly contacted me by phone and text, offering additional high-value products in lieu of a refund. I believe this was intended to undermine the active dispute I filed with my credit card company. I submitted evidence, including time-stamped photos of the unused products and screenshots of messages from the business. My bank denied the dispute twice, citing insufficient information despite the lack of documentation proving I agreed to the transaction. 

To this day, I have not signed any agreement, was not shown the full total at time of purchase, and continue to hold all products sealed and unused. XXXX XXXX business practices are part of a documented pattern of coercive and deceptive sales tactics, with similar consumer complaints reported publicly across multiple locations.
